abstract: While Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers have significantly advanced remote sensing change detection (CD), both architectures possess inherent limitations. CNNs are often constrained by a localized receptive field that struggles to capture broad spatial contexts, whereas Transformers suffer from high computational costs that impede their efficiency on large-scale datasets. In this paper, we explore the potential of the State Space Model-based Mamba architecture—which has recently demonstrated state-of-the-art performance in natural language processing—to address these challenges. We introduce three tailored frameworks: MambaBCD, MambaSCD, and MambaBDA, designed for binary change detection, semantic change detection, and building damage assessment, respectively. These frameworks utilize a Visual Mamba encoder to extract comprehensive global spatial context from input imagery. To decode changes, we propose three specialized spatio-temporal modeling mechanisms that leverage the unique attributes of the Mamba architecture to foster interaction between multi-temporal features. Evaluated across five benchmark datasets, our Mamba-based frameworks consistently outperform existing CNN and Transformer methods without the need for complex training heuristics. Furthermore, our experiments indicate that these architectures exhibit superior robustness to degraded data. The codebase is available at https://github.com/ChenHongruixuan/MambaCD.
